description_long: This bungalow features a broad, double-tiered hip roof with terracotta-colored tiles, providing extensive overhangs that shade the perimeter. The structure sits on a solid, light gray concrete base, with walls finished in a warm reddish-brown hue and accented by white trim. Large, rectangular windows are distributed along the sides, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The building's footprint is square, and the roof's layered design adds architectural interest and protection from the elements. There is no visible porch or veranda, and the entry points are subtly integrated into the facade. The overall style is modern with traditional influences, emphasizing simplicity and functionality.
description: A square bungalow with a double-tiered terracotta hip roof, reddish-brown walls, and large rectangular windows on a concrete base. The design is modern with traditional elements and lacks a visible porch.
description_view_0: This view highlights the front facade with a visible entry door and several large windows beneath the wide, overhanging roof.
description_view_1: The left side is shown, featuring more windows and the continuation of the double-tiered roof, with the entry door partially visible.
description_view_2: The rear of the bungalow is visible, showing a uniform window arrangement and the solid concrete base.
description_view_3: The right side is displayed, emphasizing the symmetry of the window placement and the expansive roof overhangs.
